title: Lannea lagdoensis
taxonomy: Lannea lagdoensis belongs to the family Anacardiaceae, a group of flowering plants commonly known as the cashew or poison ivy family. This family is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world and includes species such as the mango and the cashew nut tree. Lannea lagdoensis is also classified as a member of the genus Lannea, which is a group of deciduous trees native to Africa. The species is further classified as a member of the Anacardium clade, a group of plants that are closely related to the cashew and mango. Overall, the classification of Lannea lagdoensis highlights its unique position within the Anacardiaceae family and its distinct characteristics as a species within the genus Lannea.
